svg.text: Chat / Multi-Agent Response Architecture — Datavue vs Datacon
C:\Users\pc\Desktop\datavue (reference) compared to C:\Users\pc\Desktop\datacon (current implementation) — frontend, backend, and data layers
DATAVUE — REFERENCE ARCHITECTURE
DATACON — CURRENT IMPLEMENTATION
Chat.jsx — single-file React (CRA) page
• AgentBadge · ChartBlock — recharts bar/line; ComposedChart shades lower→upper as a translucent CI Area band
• TableBlock · CitationChip → citation drawer (document title, filename, chunk_index, snippet)
• RecommendationsBlock — numbered cards: rationale, expected_impact, effort badge, owner, per-item citations
• ResultCard (one per agent) → AssistantMessage; single "Was this helpful?" vote per whole turn
• fetch() opens SSE directly against FastAPI — no intermediate API gateway
↓ POST /api/chat/stream (fetch + ReadableStream, SSE)
FastAPI server.py — POST /api/chat/stream (SSE)
• classify_intent() keyword-scores the question → 1+ of descriptive/diagnostic/predictive/prescriptive (agents.py)
• _prepare_chat_context(): Mongo sales/churn/tickets/renewals + keyword/vector RAG over uploaded PDFs + forecast.py (Holt-Winters)
• stream_agent(): litellm.acompletion(stream=True) — LLM asked to emit ONE JSON blob (insight+chart+table+recs+confidence)
• Frontend renders the raw partial JSON text while "drafting"; _extract_json() brace-balances it once the stream ends
• SSE events: start · routed · agent_start · agent_delta (raw JSON text) · agent_done (parsed) · done · error
⚠ Trade-off: chart / table / recommendations come FROM the LLM's own JSON, not from a real query engine
• JWT (cookie-based) auth; role check (viewer cannot manage connectors/uploads)
• Same FastAPI process also serves connectors, data-source upload + chunking, and forecast endpoints
↓ persists to MongoDB directly (single process, single hop)
MongoDB
• conversations · messages · feedback (per-message up/down vote)
• data_sources + chunks (uploaded PDFs/CSV, chunked + vector-indexed for RAG citations)
• sales_by_quarter / monthly_churn / support_tickets / renewals_pipeline (seeded structured layer)
• connectors (BigQuery/MySQL/Postgres/Snowflake/SQLite/Mongo/HTTP-CSV) synced into a warehouse table
ChatPage.tsx + AgentVisualization.tsx + AgentChart.tsx (React + Vite)
• Per-intent bubbles — one turn can fan out to several agents; live LLM model picker (Qwen 3.7 Plus)
• CorrelationTag · DataTable · Citations/CitationChip → citation drawer (same shape as Datavue)
• ① ActionsTable — plain grid: title / impact / effort / owner only. No rationale, no expected_impact, no per-item citations
• ② AgentChart line — CI shown only as PROJECTED / 95% CI / GROWTH stat tiles; no shaded band on the chart itself
• Per-agent-message vote (finer-grained than Datavue's once-per-turn "Was this helpful?")
↓ POST /api/chat/stream (fetch + ReadableStream, SSE) — to NestJS, not the AI service directly
NestJS ChatController (app/api/src/chat) — hop 1 of 2
• JWT auth + RBAC guard — @RequirePermissions("ask_agents")
• Prisma/Postgres: conversations, messages, feedback; proxies POST → AI service /internal/chat/stream
• Re-streams SSE frames live to the browser; parses agent_done frames on the side, persists Message rows after "end"
↓ axios stream proxy to Python AI service (separate process)
Python AI service — chat_router.py (app/ai) — hop 2 of 2
• route() keyword-classifies the question → one or more intents (app/agents/router.py)
• Per intent, prepare() runs REAL generated+executed SQL via query_engine/executor.answer_question — deterministic, not LLM-invented
• ③ descriptive.prepare(): auto-charts ONLY when the whole result is exactly 2 columns, 2–20 rows — can't derive a
status-distribution chart alongside a wider raw-records table (the leads-table + status-chart pattern)
• llm.compose_stream() streams ONLY the clean prose insight — text deltas are display-ready, nothing to parse
• SSE: agents · agent_start · agent_delta (clean text) · agent_done (deterministic payload) · done
↓ persists via NestJS (hop 1) to Postgres; reads connected sources + Chroma directly
PostgreSQL (Prisma) + connected sources + Chroma
• conversations / messages / feedback (Postgres via Prisma)
• query_engine executes generated SQL against connected SQL/Mongo/BigQuery sources (real data, e.g. a Leads table)
• Chroma vector store for document RAG citations (app/ai/app/rag)
• Two-hop persistence: NestJS owns chat rows; the AI service owns query execution + retrieval
Gaps in Datacon's Chat UI vs Datavue
①
Prescriptive recommendations render as a bare data grid
ActionsTable only shows title / impact / effort / owner — no rationale, no expected_impact narrative, and no per-recommendation
citation chips like Datavue's numbered RecommendationsBlock cards (each linking back to the sources that justify it).
②
Predictive chart has no visual confidence-interval band
AgentChart's line chart shows the CI only as text stat tiles (PROJECTED / 95% CI / GROWTH) below the chart; Datavue shades the
lower→upper range directly on the chart itself as a translucent recharts Area layer — the uncertainty is visible, not just numeric.
③
Descriptive auto-chart is limited to exactly-2-column results
_looks_categorical() only fires when the whole query result is 2 columns and 2–20 rows, so it can't add a secondary "count by
status" distribution chart next to a wider raw-records table — the exact leads-table + status-chart pattern shown in the reference.
✓ Not a gap — a Datacon advantage:
Agents stream clean, display-ready prose (compose_stream) instead of Datavue's raw partial-JSON-while-drafting text, and every payload comes from a real
executed SQL query (query_engine) rather than an LLM inventing table/chart data inside its own JSON response — keep this design, don't regress it.
